name: Flow3D

behavior._note: Reference globals.behavior used a compile-time #define BEHAVIOR (7-way computeRotationBias dispatch, once per agent per frame). Per PORTING-GUIDE, ported as an int uniform branched at runtime with [branch] (same as the WGSL const-fold path; all variants kept). The runtime sets BEHAVIOR by reference name.

passes.name: copy
passes._comment: Copy decayed trail to write buffer before deposit so hardware blending lands correctly after ping-pong.

volumeCache._role: Declared volume cache (pooled, default 32x1024 atlas = 32 slices of 32x32). Not referenced by flow3d's own passes; present for the 3D resource model.

geoBuffer._role: outputGeo buffer (xyz=normal, w=depth). NOT written by flow3d; populated by a DOWNSTREAM render3d/renderLit3d raymarch that consumes the blended volume.

global_flow3d_state1._role: PERSISTENT agent state-surface ([x, y, z, rotRand] — 3D voxel position in [0,volSize) + per-agent rotation random). Updated in place by the agent pass MRT (SV_Target0). The deposit vertex stage Loads x/y/z to scatter into the trail atlas. isStateSurface=true (name contains 'state') → end-of-frame bindings persist, no swap.

global_flow3d_state2._role: PERSISTENT agent state-surface ([r, g, b, seed] — agent color + seed). Updated by the agent pass MRT (SV_Target1); rgb read by the deposit vertex stage as the scattered point color. isStateSurface=true.

global_flow3d_state3._role: PERSISTENT agent state-surface ([age, initialized, theta, phi] — age, init flag, spherical heading angles). Updated by the agent pass MRT (SV_Target2). isStateSurface=true.

global_flow3d_trail._role: PERSISTENT trail-volume feedback surface (2D atlas, volumeSize x volumeSize^2). diffuse multiplies by persistence (intensity/100), copy blits it to the post-ping-pong write buffer, deposit additively scatters agent colors into it (drawMode:'points', blend:true → Blend One One, count=262144 fixed), blend reads it. Reads its own prior 'global_' output so it persists frame-to-frame (runtime double-buffers/ping-pongs; reference 04 §10.2/§10.7). NOT an isStateSurface (no _xyz/_vel/_rgba suffix, no 'state') but feedback read+write of the same key keeps it alive.

global_flow3d_blended._role: Output volume (outputTex3d). blend writes input-volume*inputIntensity + trail clamped to [0,1] here. Consumed downstream by render3d/renderLit3d (raymarch) or another 3D filter.

feedback._comment: 3D / RENDER tier agent-based flow field (hardest tier: MRT agent state in 2D textures, points-scatter deposit into a 3D VOLUME ATLAS, feedback). 5 passes per frame: agent (program 'agent', fullscreen over the 512x512 state grid, MRT drawBuffers:3 → outState1/2/3), diffuse (program 'diffuse', fullscreen over the trail atlas, decays by intensity/100), copy (program 'copy', fullscreen blit of decayed trail to the post-swap write buffer), deposit (program 'deposit', drawMode:'points', count=262144 FIXED, blend:true → Blend One One; vertex stage Loads agent 3D position from state1, maps to atlas NDC (atlasY = y + floor(z)*volSize), scatters state2.rgb), blend (program 'blend', fullscreen over the atlas, writes input-volume*inputIntensity + trail → blended output volume). Volumes are PRIVATE 'global_' atlas surfaces sized (volumeSize) x (volumeSize^2), rgba16f (NOT the shared 64x4096 vol0..7). The atlas (u,v)->(x,y,z) mapping x=u, y=v%volSize, z=v/volSize is reproduced exactly in sampleVolume (manual 8-tap Load+lerp trilinear) and the deposit vertex stage. BEHAVIOR was a compile-time #define in the reference; ported as an int uniform branched at runtime. `time` (agent.wgsl binding 7) is the engine global normalized time. geoBuffer (outputGeo) is declared but written by a downstream render3d/renderLit3d raymarch, not here. The deposit pass is DrawProcedural(Points, 262144); its vertex stage Loads agent state (SM4.5); D3D points = 1px matching reference gl_PointSize=1. No '_iteration' uniform; no repeat.

_shaderGraph: SKIPPED — 3D / multi-pass / geometry (points-scatter) effect. Per PORTING-GUIDE it ships as a runtime-rendered Texture2D volume atlas, not a Shader Graph Custom Function node.
